message.content:
Read `./.claude/progress.txt`, pick the most important pending tasks, execute it, update the file, commit, and stop.

'./.claude/progress.txt' is:
- your persistent memory.
- Important errors must be registered in order to avoid expending tokens again.
- Never run tasks not registered in it.
- Register new tasks if needed, respecting the current structure.


WORKFLOW:
0. Understand Project Facts from CLAUDE.md
1. Read ./.claude/progress.txt and identify the most important pending task;
   - Specific task prioritization is indicated/tagged/prefixed with '[P'+number+']'
   - Tasks tagged with '[P0]' must be done before anything else.
   - Tasks tagged with P bigger than 999 (e.g. '[P1000]'), are low-priority 
   tasks, they should be done when possible, but they must be done 
   (they are not ignorable); If the task takes longer and there are other 
   non-low-priority tasks, document progress and revert the status to "- [ ]".
   
2. If NO pending tasks exist, signal completion (see OUTPUT STATUS below) and stop
3. If there are tasks, mark it as IN_PROGRESS and then sequentially run:
   a. Evaluate scope: if the task involves more than ~3 files or multiple concerns,
      break it into smaller sub-tasks in progress.txt BEFORE starting. Each sub-task
      must be completable in a single iteration (small, atomic, committable change).
   b. Execute task (respecting current project structure and ./.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md)
   c. Launch 1 review agents to deeply review the work, giving
      the entire context of the task, simulating pull request.
   d. Review output and consider improvements
   e. Implement corrections if needed
   f. Update progress.txt marking task as completed
   g. Commit task changes (not include unrelated code)
   h. IF THE COMMIT FAILS (pre-commit hook / lint error):
      - Add a [P0] priority task to progress.txt describing the exact errors
      - Retry the commit with `git commit --no-verify` to save your work
      - The NEXT iteration will fix the lint errors as its top priority
      - Do NOT spend time fixing lint in this iteration — save tokens for the next one
   i. Output status block (see below)
   j. Stop

- Nenhuma tarefa deve ser considerada completa sem testar com Playwright MVP 
  e tirar screenshots para visualização e visualizar.
  
OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:

RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }

Examples:
- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }

CRITICAL (THINK BEFORE ACTING):
1. The FIRST thing you MUST do is write your understanding of each WORKFLOW statement
2. The SECOND thing you MUST do is write your understanding of the work in progress described in progress.txt
3. Only AFTER completing steps 1 and 2, proceed with the task execution
4. If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
5. Only set "exit": true when ALL tasks in progress.txt are completed
6. For @semparar/* packages, read the real js code because some types are not updated correctly.
7. Never remove tasks, unless to update status;
8. Never include workarounds and add tasks to fix all you found ('as any' for example is a workaround)

   709→=== PHASE 6: RFC MISSING IMPLEMENTATIONS ======================================
   710→================================================================================
   711→
   712→NOTE: These tasks implement RFC features that have types defined (SP_0002.0)
   713→but no runtime code. They complete the SDUI engine to full RFC spec.
   714→
   715→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   716→
   717→# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
   718→- [ ] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.
   719→
   720→Context:
   721→- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
   722→  and, or, ! (logical)
   723→- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
   724→  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
   725→- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
   726→- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
   727→- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
   728→  retornam false")
   729→- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
   730→  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
   731→- Must be pure function, no side effects
   732→- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/
   733→
   734→Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
   735→comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.
   736→
   737→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   738→
   739→# SP_0006.1 — Component Visibility & Button Enabled Conditionals
   740→- [ ] Wire the JSON Logic evaluator into ScreenRenderer for component
   741→      visibility and button enabled state.
   742→
   743→Context:
   744→- SDUIComponent already has optional `visible: SDUIConditional` field
   745→- ScreenRenderer currently ignores `visible` — should skip rendering when
   746→  evaluateRule returns false
   747→- ButtonAdapter should process `enabled: { rule: SDUIRule }` in props —
   748→  disable button when rule evaluates to false
   749→- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
   750→- Update existing ScreenRenderer.test.tsx and adapters.test.tsx
   751→- Add Storybook demo showing conditional visibility toggle
   752→
   753→Deliverable: Components with `visible` conditionals are hidden/shown based on
   754→context. Buttons with `enabled` rules are disabled/enabled dynamically.
   755→
   756→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   757→
   758→# SP_0006.2 — Input Validation Rules
   759→- [ ] Implement validation rule execution for SDUI input components.
   760→
   761→Context:
   762→- RFC section 3.5 defines validation pattern:
   763→  { "validation": { "rules": [{ "rule": {...}, "message": "error text" }] } }
   764→- Types exist: SDUIValidationRule, SDUIValidation
   765→- PointsInputAdapter has textError prop but no validation integration
   766→- Needs: validateField(value, validation: SDUIValidation, context): string | null
   767→  (returns first failing rule's message, or null if valid)
   768→- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
   769→- Should integrate with PointsInputAdapter's error display
   770→- Scope: validation utility function only. Form-level orchestration (when to
   771→  trigger: blur/change/submit) is a separate concern for action handlers
   772→- Add Storybook demo showing validation in action
   773→
   774→Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/validation.ts with validateField() +
   775→integration into PointsInputAdapter + unit tests.
   776→
   777→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   778→
   779→# SP_0006.3 — Rich Text Adapter
   780→- [ ] Implement the rich_text component adapter described in RFC section 3.3.
   781→
   782→Context:
   783→- RFC defines rich text as array of typed nodes:
   784→  - text: { type: "text", value: string, bold?: boolean, italic?: boolean, color?: string }
   785→  - link: { type: "link", text: string, to: string, trackEvent?: string }
   786→  - icon: { type: "icon", name: string, color?: string, size?: number }
   787→  - linebreak: { type: "linebreak" }
   788→- Types already defined: SDUIRichText, SDUITextNode, SDUILinkNode, SDUIIconNode,
   789→  SDUILineBreakNode (in models/index.ts)
   790→- Should render inline — all nodes in a single wrapping Text component
   791→  (React Native supports nested Text for inline formatting)
   792→- Links should use DS Text with underline style (similar to LinkAdapter)
   793→- Icons use DS Icon component inline
   794→- Linebreak nodes render as `\n` inside Text (not DOM br element —
   795→  React Native Text supports newline characters)
   796→- Register as "rich_text" type in adapter registry
   797→- Add mock payload using rich_text to test inline formatting
   798→
   799→Deliverable: RichTextAdapter registered in SDUI registry + unit tests +
   800→Storybook demo showing mixed bold/link/icon inline text.
   801→
   802→--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0001.0 — Setup Storybook
- [DONE] Configure Storybook so this MFE can render screens independently of the
SuperApp.
  > Storybook 8.6.17 with @storybook/react-native-web-vite running on port 6006.
  > Custom Vite plugins: jsxInJsPlugin, dtsStubPlugin, circularFixPlugin, cjsToEsmPlugin.
  > ThemeProvider decorator in preview.tsx. Zustand store mocked in .storybook/mocks/store.ts.
  > SmokeTest story renders Text, Button, Box from DS successfully.

Context:
- This is a React Native MFE (package.json: react 18.2.0, react-native 0.73.6)
- The design system (@semparar/design-system-app ^3.39.15) already has Storybook
  configured — its setup can be used as reference if the executing agent needs it
- Babel aliases: ~ → ./src/ (babel.config.js line 27)
- TS alias: ~/store → ./@types/store.d.ts (tsconfig.json line 11) — this is a
  module augmentation for the SuperApp's Zustand store, needs mocking
- DS components depend on design tokens — may need a ThemeProvider or similar

Deliverable: `npm run storybook` opens a browser with a smoke test story that
renders at least one DS component (e.g., Button or Text from @semparar/design-system-app).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0001.1 — Validate DS components in Storybook
- [DONE] Prove that the design system components actually used in voucher screens
      render correctly in the Storybook web context.
  > 8/10 DS components render in Storybook web context.
  > BackDropBottom (Modalize) and Loading (Lottie) require native modules — cannot render on web.
  > SDUI adapters should provide web-safe fallbacks for these two.
  > Full validation story at src/__stories__/DSComponentValidation.stories.tsx.

Context:
- The voucher screens use these DS components: Text, Button, Box, InputBox,
  Alert, Icon, ListTextIcon, SeparatorHorizontal, BackDropBottom, Loading
- See existing usage in:
  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/view.tsx (form with inputs, balance, button)
  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/view.tsx (review, success, error states)
  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx (list items with icons)
- Some components may not render in web — document which ones work and which don't

Deliverable: A story page rendering each DS component listed above. Annotate any
that fail or need workarounds.

# SP_XGH986 Feature Planning & Ideation
- [DONE] Brainstorming & task creation based on ideation frameworks.
  > Applied SCAMPER (Substitute/Combine/Adapt/Eliminate) + Jobs-to-be-Done
  > to identify DX improvements for the rewards program MFE.
  > Core insight: "developers need to validate UI changes without deploying
  > to the SuperApp" → Storybook as the primary inner-loop dev tool.
  > Tasks SP_0003.x (foundation), SP_0004.x (screens), SP_0005.x (SDUI expansion)
  > created below.
  >
  > Ideation frameworks used:
  > - SCAMPER: What can we Substitute (SuperApp → Storybook), Combine
  >   (JSON editor + live preview), Adapt (SDUI to existing screens),
  >   Eliminate (API/login dependencies)?
  > - JTBD: "When I change a screen, I want to see the result immediately
  >   without navigating through the SuperApp login/navigation flow"
  > - DX Inner Loop: Optimize write→see→validate cycle from minutes to seconds
  > - Apple Creative Selection (Ken Kocienda): Demo-driven development —
  >   build it, see it, iterate. Storybook IS the demo environment.
  >
  > References:
  > - SCAMPER: https://www.interaction-design.org/literature/article/learn-how-to-use-the-best-ideation-methods-scamper
  > - DX Framework: https://arxiv.org/abs/2205.06352
  > - DevEx (ACM): https://queue.acm.org/detail.cfm?id=3595878
  > - Creative Selection (book): https://creativeselection.io/

# SP_0003.2 — Storybook JSON Editor Addon (IndexedDB Persistence)
- [DONE] Create a custom Storybook addon panel that allows editing the SDUI JSON
      payload in real-time, with automatic persistence to IndexedDB.
  > 6 files in .storybook/addons/json-editor/:
  >   register.tsx (addon registration), JsonEditorPanel.tsx (editor UI),
  >   useSDUIJsonEditor.ts (preview-side hook), useIndexedDB.ts (persistence),
  >   constants.ts (shared IDs/events), index.ts (barrel exports).
  > Features: textarea with monospace font, Format/Reset buttons, debounced
  >   auto-save to IndexedDB keyed by story ID, JSON validation with inline errors,
  >   "edited" badge when payload differs from default, live preview via channel events.
  > Integrated in main.ts addons array + used by SDUIDemoVoucher stories.
  > Review fixes applied: IDB error handling (.catch), db.close() on cleanup,
  >   debounce timer cleanup on unmount.
  > Screenshot validated: json-editor-validated.png.

Context:
- Every SDUI story should have a companion JSON editor panel
- The editor should:
  a. Show the current SDUIScreen JSON in a textarea or code editor
  b. Parse and validate JSON on change (show errors inline)
  c. Apply changes live to the ScreenRenderer preview
  d. Auto-save to IndexedDB keyed by story ID so edits persist across refreshes
  e. Have a "Reset to Default" button to restore the original mock payload
- Implementation approach:
  a. Create .storybook/addons/json-editor/ with:
     - register.tsx (Storybook addon registration)
     - JsonEditorPanel.tsx (the editor UI component)
     - useIndexedDB.ts (IndexedDB read/write hook)
  b. Register as a panel addon in .storybook/main.ts
  c. Use Storybook's addons API: addons.register(), addons.addPanel()
  d. Communicate with stories via Storybook channel events
- Reference: https://storybook.js.org/docs/addons/writing-addons
- The editor should work with ALL SDUI stories (current and future)
- Consider using a simple <textarea> with monospace font for MVP — no need for
  a full Monaco editor initially
- IndexedDB key format: `sdui-json-${storyId}`

Deliverable: A "JSON Editor" panel tab in Storybook that edits SDUI payloads
live with IndexedDB persistence. Works with all SDUI stories.

================================================================================
=== PHASE 6: RFC MISSING IMPLEMENTATIONS ======================================
================================================================================

NOTE: These tasks implement RFC features that have types defined (SP_0002.0)
but no runtime code. They complete the SDUI engine to full RFC spec.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
- [ ] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
  retornam false")
- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.1 — Component Visibility & Button Enabled Conditionals
- [ ] Wire the JSON Logic evaluator into ScreenRenderer for component
      visibility and button enabled state.

Context:
- SDUIComponent already has optional `visible: SDUIConditional` field
- ScreenRenderer currently ignores `visible` — should skip rendering when
  evaluateRule returns false
- ButtonAdapter should process `enabled: { rule: SDUIRule }` in props —
  disable button when rule evaluates to false
- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
- Update existing ScreenRenderer.test.tsx and adapters.test.tsx
- Add Storybook demo showing conditional visibility toggle

Deliverable: Components with `visible` conditionals are hidden/shown based on
context. Buttons with `enabled` rules are disabled/enabled dynamically.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.2 — Input Validation Rules
- [ ] Implement validation rule execution for SDUI input components.

Context:
- RFC section 3.5 defines validation pattern:
  { "validation": { "rules": [{ "rule": {...}, "message": "error text" }] } }
- Types exist: SDUIValidationRule, SDUIValidation
- PointsInputAdapter has textError prop but no validation integration
- Needs: validateField(value, validation: SDUIValidation, context): string | null
  (returns first failing rule's message, or null if valid)
- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
- Should integrate with PointsInputAdapter's error display
- Scope: validation utility function only. Form-level orchestration (when to
  trigger: blur/change/submit) is a separate concern for action handlers
- Add Storybook demo showing validation in action

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/validation.ts with validateField() +
integration into PointsInputAdapter + unit tests.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.3 — Rich Text Adapter
- [ ] Implement the rich_text component adapter described in RFC section 3.3.

Context:
- RFC defines rich text as array of typed nodes:
  - text: { type: "text", value: string, bold?: boolean, italic?: boolean, color?: string }
  - link: { type: "link", text: string, to: string, trackEvent?: string }
  - icon: { type: "icon", name: string, color?: string, size?: number }
  - linebreak: { type: "linebreak" }
- Types already defined: SDUIRichText, SDUITextNode, SDUILinkNode, SDUIIconNode,
  SDUILineBreakNode (in models/index.ts)
- Should render inline — all nodes in a single wrapping Text component
  (React Native supports nested Text for inline formatting)
- Links should use DS Text with underline style (similar to LinkAdapter)
- Icons use DS Icon component inline
- Linebreak nodes render as `\n` inside Text (not DOM br element —
  React Native Text supports newline characters)
- Register as "rich_text" type in adapter registry
- Add mock payload using rich_text to test inline formatting

Deliverable: RichTextAdapter registered in SDUI registry + unit tests +
Storybook demo showing mixed bold/link/icon inline text.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the context. Let me provide a detailed PR review.

---

## PR Review: SP_0004.2 — PointsDetail Screen Story

### **BLOCKING Issues**

1. **Line 195 - Modal rendering logic is incorrect**
   - The modal should NOT be conditionally rendered inside `ScrollView`. In the real implementation (view.tsx:179-250), `BackDropBottom` is rendered outside `BasicScreen` as a sibling component.
   - **Current**: Modal appears inline with scroll content when `showModal === true`
   - **Expected**: Modal should be rendered at root level (outside ScrollView) and controlled via state
   - **Fix**: Move `ReportProblemModal` outside `ScrollView`, wrap in conditional based on `showModal`

2. **Line 264 - Type casting loses safety**
   - `transaction.type === 'REFUNDED'` condition duplicates view.tsx logic, but the color logic has a subtle issue: the ternary produces `'PLUS_COLOR_02' | 'INTENSE_COLOR_05'`, but the `as const` assertion on line 264 only applies to the false branch.
   - Compare to view.tsx:144-146 which correctly applies the assertion.

### **MINOR Issues**

3. **Lines 150-212 - ReportProblemModal component deviates from production**
   - Missing `pd="innerSmall"` wrapper around CardActivity elements (view.tsx:193)
   - Box padding structure differs: story has direct `pd="innerSmall"` on root, but production has nested structure with specific padding placement
   - **Impact**: Visual inconsistency in spacing

4. **Line 238 - Missing `gainType` state context**
   - The story doesn't demonstrate the difference between "Compra feita em..." vs "Pontos usados em..." highlight text based on `gainType`. This is a key variation in `getFormattedHighlightText` (utils.ts:178)
   - Consider adding a CASHBACK RESCUE mock to show this variation properly

5. **Lines 284-335 - TAG content rendering could miss edge cases**
   - The inline TAG content logic (lines 284-335) duplicates the renderTagContent() function from view.tsx but is not extracted as a function
   - **Not blocking**, but reduces maintainability if utils.ts logic changes

### **NITS**

6. **Line 27 - Import order**
   - `moment` import from `../services/moment` comes after utils, breaking alphabetical/layer order convention seen in other stories

7. **Lines 29-136 - Mock data lacks diversity**
   - `MOCK_TAG_TRANSACTION` includes all vehicle fields (Marca, Modelo, Placa), but doesn't test the fallback scenarios in `getVehicleInfo` (utils.ts:94-128):
     - Only placa available (line 114)
     - Only origem field available (line 124)
   - Consider adding a mock with partial vehicle data

8. **Line 363 - Missing description in argTypes**
   - Story variants are complete (6 types), but argTypes description could be more explicit about what each transaction type demonstrates

9. **Line 196 - Hardcoded typo exists in production**
   - `"*Atendimento de segunda à sexta..."` uses incorrect preposition `à` (should be `a`). This matches production (view.tsx:234), so it's not a story bug, but worth noting for future fix.

---

### **Missing States/Edge Cases**

- **PENDING state**: No mock for `status: 'PENDING'` transactions
- **Partial vehicle info**: As noted above, missing test for placa-only or origem-only scenarios
- **Empty/null descriptions**: No test for `description === ''` or missing icon
- **Loading state**: Unlike other stories (Extract, Points), there's no loading skeleton variant

---

### **Type Safety**

- Line 15: Import from `extract.model` is correct
- Line 16: `formatToCurrency` from `../utils/functions` is correct
- Line 17-26: All utils are properly imported
- Type safety is generally good, except for the casting issue on line 255

---

### **Story Metadata Completeness**

- ✅ Title: 'Screens/PointsDetail' follows convention
- ✅ Decorators: NavigationDecorator applied
- ✅ ArgTypes: Present with select control
- ✅ 6 named stories covering major transaction types
- ❌ No `parameters` metadata for viewport/layout (minor)

---

### **Consistency with Existing Stories**

**Compared to ExtractScreen.stories.tsx & PointsScreen.stories.tsx:**

- ✅ Uses same manual recreation pattern for native components
- ✅ DS components used directly
- ✅ Proper seeding pattern (though simpler since no store manipulation needed)
- ❌ Missing loading state variant (both other stories have this)
- ❌ Missing error state variant (PointsScreen has this)
- ⚠️ Modal implementation pattern differs from production structure

---

### **Severity Summary**

- **Blocking**: 2 (modal rendering, type casting)
- **Minor**: 3 (spacing, highlight text variation, code duplication)
- **Nit**: 4 (import order, mock diversity, descriptions, production typo)

---

### **Recommended Actions**

1. Fix modal rendering by moving it outside ScrollView
2. Correct pointsColor type casting to match view.tsx exactly
3. Align ReportProblemModal padding structure with production
4. Add loading/error state variants for completeness
5. Extract TAG content rendering to helper function

   - **Expected**: Modal should be rendered at root level (outside ScrollView) and controlled via state
   - **Fix**: Move `ReportProblemModal` outside `ScrollView`, wrap in conditional based on `showModal`

2. **Line 264 - Type casting loses safety**
   - `transaction.type === 'REFUNDED'` condition duplicates view.tsx logic, but the color logic has a subtle issue: the ternary produces `'PLUS_COLOR_02' | 'INTENSE_COLOR_05'`, but the `as const` assertion on line 264 only applies to the false branch.
   - Compare to view.tsx:144-146 which correctly applies the assertion.

### **MINOR Issues**

3. **Lines 150-212 - ReportProblemModal component deviates from production**
   - Missing `pd="innerSmall"` wrapper around CardActivity elements (view.tsx:193)
   - Box padding structure differs: story has direct `pd="innerSmall"` on root, but production has nested structure with specific padding placement
   - **Impact**: Visual inconsistency in spacing

4. **Line 238 - Missing `gainType` state context**
   - The story doesn't demonstrate the difference between "Compra feita em..." vs "Pontos usados em..." highlight text based on `gainType`. This is a key variation in `getFormattedHighlightText` (utils.ts:178)
   - Consider adding a CASHBACK RESCUE mock to show this variation properly

5. **Lines 284-335 - TAG content rendering could miss edge cases**
   - The inline TAG content logic (lines 284-335) duplicates the renderTagContent() function from view.tsx but is not extracted as a function
   - **Not blocking**, but reduces maintainability if utils.ts logic changes

### **NITS**

6. **Line 27 - Import order**
   - `moment` import from `../services/moment` comes after utils, breaking alphabetical/layer order convention seen in other stories

7. **Lines 29-136 - Mock data lacks diversity**
   - `MOCK_TAG_TRANSACTION` includes all vehicle fields (Marca, Modelo, Placa), but doesn't test the fallback scenarios in `getVehicleInfo` (utils.ts:94-128):
     - Only placa available (line 114)
     - Only origem field available (line 124)
   - Consider adding a mock with partial vehicle data

8. **Line 363 - Missing description in argTypes**
   - Story variants are complete (6 types), but argTypes description could be more explicit about what each transaction type demonstrates

9. **Line 196 - Hardcoded typo exists in production**
   - `"*Atendimento de segunda à sexta..."` uses incorrect preposition `à` (should be `a`). This matches production (view.tsx:234), so it's not a story bug, but worth noting for future fix.

---

### **Missing States/Edge Cases**

- **PENDING state**: No mock for `status: 'PENDING'` transactions
- **Partial vehicle info**: As noted above, missing test for placa-only or origem-only scenarios
- **Empty/null descriptions**: No test for `description === ''` or missing icon
- **Loading state**: Unlike other stories (Extract, Points), there's no loading skeleton variant

---

### **Type Safety**

- Line 15: Import from `extract.model` is correct
- Line 16: `formatToCurrency` from `../utils/functions` is correct
- Line 17-26: All utils are properly imported
- Type safety is generally good, except for the casting issue on line 255

---

### **Story Metadata Completeness**

- ✅ Title: 'Screens/PointsDetail' follows convention
- ✅ Decorators: NavigationDecorator applied
- ✅ ArgTypes: Present with select control
- ✅ 6 named stories covering major transaction types
- ❌ No `parameters` metadata for viewport/layout (minor)

---

### **Consistency with Existing Stories**

**Compared to ExtractScreen.stories.tsx & PointsScreen.stories.tsx:**

- ✅ Uses same manual recreation pattern for native components
- ✅ DS components used directly
- ✅ Proper seeding pattern (though simpler since no store manipulation needed)
- ❌ Missing loading state variant (both other stories have this)
- ❌ Missing error state variant (PointsScreen has this)
- ⚠️ Modal implementation pattern differs from production structure

---

### **Severity Summary**

- **Blocking**: 2 (modal rendering, type casting)
- **Minor**: 3 (spacing, highlight text variation, code duplication)
- **Nit**: 4 (import order, mock diversity, descriptions, production typo)

---

### **Recommended Actions**

1. Fix modal rendering by moving it outside ScrollView
2. Correct pointsColor type casting to match view.tsx exactly
3. Align ReportProblemModal padding structure with production
4. Add loading/error state variants for completeness
5. Extract TAG content rendering to helper function

Good review. Let me address the blocking issues:

1. **Modal rendering** — Move it outside ScrollView (this is valid)
2. **Type casting** — The `as const` is actually correct since it only applies to the string literal, but let me align it with the source exactly

Let me fix the blocking issues.

# SP_0001.0 — Setup Storybook
- [DONE] Configure Storybook so this MFE can render screens independently of the
SuperApp.
  > Storybook 8.6.17 with @storybook/react-native-web-vite running on port 6006.
  > Custom Vite plugins: jsxInJsPlugin, dtsStubPlugin, circularFixPlugin, cjsToEsmPlugin.
  > ThemeProvider decorator in preview.tsx. Zustand store mocked in .storybook/mocks/store.ts.
  > SmokeTest story renders Text, Button, Box from DS successfully.

Context:
- This is a React Native MFE (package.json: react 18.2.0, react-native 0.73.6)
- The design system (@semparar/design-system-app ^3.39.15) already has Storybook
  configured — its setup can be used as reference if the executing agent needs it
- Babel aliases: ~ → ./src/ (babel.config.js line 27)
- TS alias: ~/store → ./@types/store.d.ts (tsconfig.json line 11) — this is a
  module augmentation for the SuperApp's Zustand store, needs mocking
- DS components depend on design tokens — may need a ThemeProvider or similar

Deliverable: `npm run storybook` opens a browser with a smoke test story that
renders at least one DS component (e.g., Button or Text from @semparar/design-system-app).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0001.1 — Validate DS components in Storybook
- [DONE] Prove that the design system components actually used in voucher screens
      render correctly in the Storybook web context.
  > 8/10 DS components render in Storybook web context.
  > BackDropBottom (Modalize) and Loading (Lottie) require native modules — cannot render on web.
  > SDUI adapters should provide web-safe fallbacks for these two.
  > Full validation story at src/__stories__/DSComponentValidation.stories.tsx.

Context:
- The voucher screens use these DS components: Text, Button, Box, InputBox,
  Alert, Icon, ListTextIcon, SeparatorHorizontal, BackDropBottom, Loading
- See existing usage in:
  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/view.tsx (form with inputs, balance, button)
  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/view.tsx (review, success, error states)
  - src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx (list items with icons)
- Some components may not render in web — document which ones work and which don't

Deliverable: A story page rendering each DS component listed above. Annotate any
that fail or need workarounds.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.0 — SDUI Core: Types & Interfaces
- [DONE] Define the TypeScript type system for the SDUI architecture described in
      .claude/rfc-conteudo-dinamico.txt (sections 3.1–3.5).
  > Types at src/services/sdui/models/index.ts (180 lines).
  > Covers: SDUIScreen, SDUIComponent, SDUIContext, SDUIRichText, SDUIRule,
  >   SDUIConditional, SDUIValidation, SDUIComponentRegistry, SDUIComponentRenderer.
  > SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES const array with all 13 voucher-relevant component types.

Context:
- Screen structure: { id, context, header, body[], footer } (RFC section 3.1)
- Components: { type, props } (RFC section 3.2)
- Rich text: { type: "rich_text", children: [nodes] } (RFC section 3.3)
- Context interpolation: {{var}} pattern (RFC section 3.4)
- Conditionals: JSON Logic subset (RFC section 3.5)
- Follow project conventions: .claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md (services architecture,
  models in Models/ folders)

Deliverable: Type definitions in src/services/sdui/models/ that contract the
entire SDUI system (Screen, Component, Context, RichText, ComponentRegistry).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.1 — SDUI Core: Interpolation Parser
- [DONE] Implement the {{var}} replacement engine from RFC section 3.4.
  > src/services/sdui/utils/interpolation.ts — 3 functions:
  >   resolveContextPath (nested path + array index support)
  >   interpolateString ({{var}} regex replacement)
  >   interpolateDeep (recursive object/array walk)
  > 24 unit tests passing (interpolation.test.ts).
  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIInterpolation.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshot validated via Playwright.

Context:
- Must resolve nested paths: {{product.name}}, {{balance.pointsFormatted}}
- Must deep-walk an object interpolating all string values in props
- Missing values should resolve gracefully (empty string, not crash)
- Existing interpolation example in RFC:
  "Voucher para {{product.name}}" + context.product.name = "Abastecimento"
  → "Voucher para Abastecimento"
- Types from SP_0002.0 should be used

Deliverable: Interpolation utility with unit tests.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.2 — SDUI Core: Component Registry & Screen Renderer
- [DONE] Build the rendering engine that turns a JSON screen definition into React
      components. This is the core of the SDUI architecture (RFC sections 3.1–3.2).
  > Registry at src/services/sdui/registry/index.ts — createRegistry, registerComponent,
  >   getRenderer, registerToDefault (immutable API + mutable default singleton).
  > ScreenRenderer at src/services/sdui/renderer/ScreenRenderer.tsx — renders header,
  >   body[], footer from SDUIScreen JSON. Applies interpolateDeep to all props.
  > FallbackComponent at src/services/sdui/renderer/FallbackComponent.tsx — visible
  >   yellow box for unknown component types (uses RN primitives for ThemeProvider safety).
  > 14 unit tests passing (registry.test.ts + ScreenRenderer.test.tsx).
  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIScreenRenderer.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshot validated via Playwright.

Context:
- Registry maps string type → React component (RFC section 3.2 "Componentes")
- Renderer receives SDUIScreen JSON, iterates body[], renders each via registry
- Must apply interpolation (SP_0002.1) to all string props using screen context
- Unknown types should render a visible fallback (not crash)
- Types from SP_0002.0, interpolation from SP_0002.1

Deliverable: A <ScreenRenderer screen={json} /> component that renders a
screen definition from JSON.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.3 — SDUI Component Adapters
- [DONE] Create adapters that bridge SDUI component types to actual DS components.
  > 11 adapters implemented: heading, text, section_heading, balance_display,
  >   points_input, button, link, alert, list_text_icon, separator, icon_text_row.
  > All registered via registerAllAdapters() in src/services/sdui/adapters/index.ts.
  > 13 unit tests passing (adapters.test.tsx).
  > Storybook story at src/__stories__/SDUIAdapters.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshots validated: sdui-adapters.png + sdui-adapters-full.png.
  > Review notes: stateful PointsInputAdapter + stubbed onPress handlers are
  >   acceptable tech debt for spike phase.

Context:
- Each adapter maps SDUI props to @semparar/design-system-app component props
- Required adapters based on voucher screen analysis (TASK.pdf + existing screens):

  | SDUI type        | DS component(s)           | Used in                    |
  |------------------|---------------------------|----------------------------|
  | heading          | Text (headingMDL)         | Form title, Review title   |
  | text             | Text (configurable)       | Labels, descriptions       |
  | balance_display  | Box + Text                | "Pontos disponíveis: X"   |
  | points_input     | InputBox                  | Points/reais fields        |
  | button           | Button                    | CTA buttons                |
  | link             | Text (pressable)          | "Exibir regras de uso"     |
  | alert            | Alert                     | Info boxes in summary      |
  | list_text_icon   | ListTextIcon              | Onboarding items, rules    |
  | separator        | SeparatorHorizontal       | Between fields             |
  | icon_text_row    | Box + Icon + Text         | Regras de uso bullet items |
  | section_heading  | Text (subtitleXXL)        | Regras section titles      |

- Register all in the default registry from SP_0002.2
- DS component validation from SP_0001.1 may affect which adapters are viable

Deliverable: All voucher-relevant adapters registered in the component registry.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.4 — Mock JSON Payloads
- [DONE] Create realistic JSON payloads for the voucher screens described in TASK.pdf.
  > 8 payloads: 4 screens (form, summary, conclusion, rules) × 2 products (Abastecimento 206, Estacionamento 205).
  > Files at src/services/sdui/mocks/ — voucherForm.ts, voucherSummary.ts, voucherConclusion.ts, voucherRules.ts.
  > Barrel export at src/services/sdui/mocks/index.ts.
  > All payloads use {{var}} interpolation for dynamic content (balance, conversion, product name).
  > Storybook validation story at src/__stories__/SDUIMockPayloads.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshot validated via Playwright (sdui-mockpayloads-full.png).
  > Review notes: icon_text_row with empty text in conclusion screen is a known spike limitation
  >   (would need a new `status_icon` type for proper centered icon rendering).

Context:
- 4 screens need payloads (see TASK.pdf pages 2–4 for visual reference):
  1. Voucher form (points/reais input) — reference: InvoiceCashbackPoints/view.tsx
  2. Voucher summary (review before confirm) — reference: InvoiceCashbackSummary/view.tsx lines 47-151
  3. Voucher conclusion (success) — reference: InvoiceCashbackSummary/view.tsx lines 154-203
  4. Regras de uso (rules) — reference: TASK.pdf page 4 screenshot
- Product codes (src/screens/Points/data/index.ts lines 62-70):
  206 = Abastecimento, 205 = Estacionamento, 207 = Seguro Auto, 208 = IPVA
- Text differences per product visible in TASK.pdf page 2:
  - Cashback: "Defina quanto cashback quer na sua fatura"
  - Voucher: "Defina o desconto para seu abastecimento"
  - Cashback: "Cashback aplicado" / Voucher: "O voucher foi criado"
  - Cashback: "Confirmar desconto" / Voucher: "Criar voucher"
- Static content (labels, titles, rules) goes in JSON. Dynamic data (balance,
  points) comes from context — not hardcoded in the payload.
- Include at least 2 product variants (Abastecimento + Estacionamento) to prove
  the same renderer handles different products via different JSON.

Deliverable: JSON payloads for 4 screens × 2 products.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0002.5 — Demo Stories: SDUI Rendering
- [DONE] Final demonstration — render all voucher screens from JSON through the SDUI
      engine in Storybook.
  > 5 stories in src/__stories__/SDUIDemoVoucher.stories.tsx:
  >   Form, Summary, Conclusion, Rules (individual with product toggle)
  >   FullFlow (combined: product toggle + step navigation across all 4 screens).
  > Product toggle proves: same ScreenRenderer + adapters, different JSON = different screens.
  > nav_header shows as yellow fallback (expected — no DS adapter for navigation).
  > Screenshots validated via Playwright (sdui-demo-form, summary, conclusion, rules, fullflow).
  > Review: no blocking issues. Hard-coded colors in toggle buttons acceptable for spike.

Context:
- Uses: ScreenRenderer (SP_0002.2), adapters (SP_0002.3), payloads (SP_0002.4)
- Each story feeds a mock JSON payload into <ScreenRenderer />
- Key demonstration: switching product context (Abastecimento → Estacionamento)
  changes the rendered screen WITHOUT changing any code — only JSON differs
- This is the spike's central proof: same code, different JSON, different screens

Deliverable: Storybook stories showing all 4 voucher screens rendered from JSON,
with product variant toggle proving reusability. This is the spike's final output.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_XGH986 Feature Planning & Ideation
- [DONE] Brainstorming & task creation based on ideation frameworks.
  > Applied SCAMPER (Substitute/Combine/Adapt/Eliminate) + Jobs-to-be-Done
  > to identify DX improvements for the rewards program MFE.
  > Core insight: "developers need to validate UI changes without deploying
  > to the SuperApp" → Storybook as the primary inner-loop dev tool.
  > Tasks SP_0003.x (foundation), SP_0004.x (screens), SP_0005.x (SDUI expansion)
  > created below.
  >
  > Ideation frameworks used:
  > - SCAMPER: What can we Substitute (SuperApp → Storybook), Combine
  >   (JSON editor + live preview), Adapt (SDUI to existing screens),
  >   Eliminate (API/login dependencies)?
  > - JTBD: "When I change a screen, I want to see the result immediately
  >   without navigating through the SuperApp login/navigation flow"
  > - DX Inner Loop: Optimize write→see→validate cycle from minutes to seconds
  > - Apple Creative Selection (Ken Kocienda): Demo-driven development —
  >   build it, see it, iterate. Storybook IS the demo environment.
  >
  > References:
  > - SCAMPER: https://www.interaction-design.org/literature/article/learn-how-to-use-the-best-ideation-methods-scamper
  > - DX Framework: https://arxiv.org/abs/2205.06352
  > - DevEx (ACM): https://queue.acm.org/detail.cfm?id=3595878
  > - Creative Selection (book): https://creativeselection.io/

================================================================================
=== PHASE 6: RFC MISSING IMPLEMENTATIONS ======================================
================================================================================

NOTE: These tasks implement RFC features that have types defined (SP_0002.0)
but no runtime code. They complete the SDUI engine to full RFC spec.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
- [ ] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
  retornam false")
- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.1 — Component Visibility & Button Enabled Conditionals
- [ ] Wire the JSON Logic evaluator into ScreenRenderer for component
      visibility and button enabled state.

Context:
- SDUIComponent already has optional `visible: SDUIConditional` field
- ScreenRenderer currently ignores `visible` — should skip rendering when
  evaluateRule returns false
- ButtonAdapter should process `enabled: { rule: SDUIRule }` in props —
  disable button when rule evaluates to false
- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
- Update existing ScreenRenderer.test.tsx and adapters.test.tsx
- Add Storybook demo showing conditional visibility toggle

Deliverable: Components with `visible` conditionals are hidden/shown based on
context. Buttons with `enabled` rules are disabled/enabled dynamically.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.2 — Input Validation Rules
- [ ] Implement validation rule execution for SDUI input components.

Context:
- RFC section 3.5 defines validation pattern:
  { "validation": { "rules": [{ "rule": {...}, "message": "error text" }] } }
- Types exist: SDUIValidationRule, SDUIValidation
- PointsInputAdapter has textError prop but no validation integration
- Needs: validateField(value, validation: SDUIValidation, context): string | null
  (returns first failing rule's message, or null if valid)
- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
- Should integrate with PointsInputAdapter's error display
- Scope: validation utility function only. Form-level orchestration (when to
  trigger: blur/change/submit) is a separate concern for action handlers
- Add Storybook demo showing validation in action

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/validation.ts with validateField() +
integration into PointsInputAdapter + unit tests.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.3 — Rich Text Adapter
- [ ] Implement the rich_text component adapter described in RFC section 3.3.

Context:
- RFC defines rich text as array of typed nodes:
  - text: { type: "text", value: string, bold?: boolean, italic?: boolean, color?: string }
  - link: { type: "link", text: string, to: string, trackEvent?: string }
  - icon: { type: "icon", name: string, color?: string, size?: number }
  - linebreak: { type: "linebreak" }
- Types already defined: SDUIRichText, SDUITextNode, SDUILinkNode, SDUIIconNode,
  SDUILineBreakNode (in models/index.ts)
- Should render inline — all nodes in a single wrapping Text component
  (React Native supports nested Text for inline formatting)
- Links should use DS Text with underline style (similar to LinkAdapter)
- Icons use DS Icon component inline
- Linebreak nodes render as `\n` inside Text (not DOM br element —
  React Native Text supports newline characters)
- Register as "rich_text" type in adapter registry
- Add mock payload using rich_text to test inline formatting

Deliverable: RichTextAdapter registered in SDUI registry + unit tests +
Storybook demo showing mixed bold/link/icon inline text.

content.content:
1→import {
     2→  BackDropBottom,
     3→  Box,
     4→  Button,
     5→  CardActivity,
     6→  DetailTransactionValue,
     7→  SeparatorHorizontal,
     8→  TagLabel,
     9→  Text,
    10→} from '@semparar/design-system-app';
    11→import { PropsModalizeRef } from '@semparar/design-system-app/src/@types/modelize';
    12→import React, { FC } from 'react';
    13→import { Linking } from 'react-native';
    14→import { eventSelectContent } from '~/constants/functions';
    15→import BasicScreen from '../../atomic/templates/BasicScreen';
    16→import { moment } from '../../services/moment';
    17→import { formatToCurrency } from '../../utils/functions';
    18→import { PointsDetailProps } from './Models';
    19→import {
    20→  getDetailScreenTitle,
    21→  getFormattedHighlightText,
    22→  getFormattedPointsValue,
    23→  getOrigin,
    24→  getPurchaseTypeLabel,
    25→  getTagSubType,
    26→  getTransactionGainType,
    27→  getVehicleInfo,
    28→} from './utils';
    29→
    30→const PointsDetailView: FC<PointsDetailProps> = ({
    31→  handleGoBack,
    32→  transaction,
    33→  modalizeRef,
    34→  handleOpenReportProblem,
    35→  handleCloseReportProblem,
    36→}) => {
    37→  const gainType = getTransactionGainType(transaction);
    38→  const tagSubType = getTagSubType(transaction);
    39→  const screenTitle = getDetailScreenTitle(gainType, transaction.type);
    40→  const vehicleInfo = getVehicleInfo(transaction);
    41→  const purchaseTypeLabel = getPurchaseTypeLabel(tagSubType);
    42→  const origin = getOrigin(transaction);
    43→  const formattedCurrencyValue = formatToCurrency(transaction.currencyValue);
    44→  const formattedPointsValue = getFormattedPointsValue(
    45→    transaction.amount,
    46→    transaction.type,
    47→  );
    48→  const cardTitle = transaction.description || '';
    49→  const highlightText = getFormattedHighlightText(
    50→    transaction.date,
    51→    gainType,
    52→    moment,
    53→  );
    54→
    55→  const renderDivider = () => {
    56→    return (
    57→      <SeparatorHorizontal
    58→        borderWidth="smaller"
    59→        borderColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02"
    60→        height={1}
    61→        width="100%"
    62→        mt="xxxxs"
    63→      />
    64→    );
    65→  };
    66→
    67→  const renderTagContent = () => {
    68→    const hasVehicleInfo = !!vehicleInfo && vehicleInfo !== '';
    69→    const shouldShowOrigin = origin && !hasVehicleInfo;
    70→    const hasPurchaseTypeLabel =
    71→      !!purchaseTypeLabel && purchaseTypeLabel !== '';
    72→
    73→    return (
    74→      <>
    75→        {renderDivider()}
    76→        {shouldShowOrigin && (
    77→          <>
    78→            <Text
    79→              type="paragraphXL"
    80→              color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
    81→              mb="xxxxs"
    82→              mt="xxxxs"
    83→            >
    84→              Origem
    85→            </Text>
    86→            <Text type="paragraphMD" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03">
    87→              {origin}
    88→            </Text>
    89→            {renderDivider()}
    90→          </>
    91→        )}
    92→        {hasVehicleInfo && (
    93→          <>
    94→            <Text
    95→              type="paragraphXL"
    96→              color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
    97→              mb="xxxxs"
    98→              mt="xxxxs"
    99→            >
   100→              Tag
   101→            </Text>
   102→            <Text type="paragraphMD" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03">
   103→              {vehicleInfo}
   104→            </Text>
   105→            {renderDivider()}
   106→          </>
   107→        )}
   108→
   109→        {hasPurchaseTypeLabel && (
   110→          <>
   111→            <Text
   112→              type="paragraphXL"
   113→              color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
   114→              mb="xxxxs"
   115→              mt="xxxxs"
   116→            >
   117→              Tipo de compra
   118→            </Text>
   119→            <Text type="paragraphMD" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03">
   120→              {purchaseTypeLabel}
   121→            </Text>
   122→          </>
   123→        )}
   124→      </>
   125→    );
   126→  };
   127→
   128→  return (
   129→    <>
   130→      <BasicScreen
   131→        title={screenTitle}
   132→        leftIconOnPress={handleGoBack}
   133→        hasScroll
   134→        errorButtonOnPress={() => {}}
   135→      >
   136→        <DetailTransactionValue
   137→          icon={transaction.icon as never}
   138→          title={cardTitle}
   139→          titleColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
   140→          iconBgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03"
   141→          points={formattedPointsValue}
   142→          bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02"
   143→          pointsColor={
   144→            transaction.type === 'CREDIT' || transaction.type === 'REFUNDED'
   145→              ? 'PLUS_COLOR_02'
   146→              : ('INTENSE_COLOR_05' as const)
   147→          }
   148→        />
   149→
   150→        <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller" mt="xxxxs" flex={1}>
   151→          <TagLabel
   152→            icon={undefined}
   153→            type="neutral"
   154→            text={highlightText}
   155→            showSpacing
   156→          />
   157→
   158→          <Box mt="xxxs" flex={1} width="100%">
   159→            <Text type="paragraphXL" color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01" mb="xxxxs">
   160→              Valor
   161→            </Text>
   162→            <Text type="paragraphMD" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03">
   163→              {formattedCurrencyValue}
   164→            </Text>
   165→
   166→            {gainType === 'TAG' && renderTagContent()}
   167→          </Box>
   168→
   169→          <Box width="100%" align="flex-end" mb="small">
   170→            <Button
   171→              type="secundaryLarge"
   172→              text="Relatar Problema"
   173→              onPress={handleOpenReportProblem}
   174→            />
   175→          </Box>
   176→        </Box>
   177→      </BasicScreen>
   178→
   179→      <BackDropBottom modalizeRef={modalizeRef as PropsModalizeRef}>
   180→        <Box align="center" width="100%" mt="xxs">
   181→          <Box align="center" width="100%">
   182→            <Text
   183→              type="paragraphXXXL"
   184→              color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
   185→              mb="xs"
   186→              align="center"
   187→            >
   188→              Central de Atendimento
   189→            </Text>
   190→          </Box>
   191→        </Box>
   192→
   193→        <Box pd="innerSmall">
   194→          <CardActivity
   195→            mt="nano"
   196→            icon="icon-contact"
   197→            title="Capitais"
   198→            description="4002 1552"
   199→            iconRight="icon-next"
   200→            showIcon
   201→            showTagLabel={false}
   202→            onPress={() => {
   203→              eventSelectContent({
   204→                content_type: 'cta',
   205→                content_id: 'telefones_capitais',
   206→                location_1: 'monetizacao',
   207→                location_2: 'hub_recompensas_spmais',
   208→                location_3: 'contato_telefones',
   209→              });
   210→              Linking.openURL('tel:40021552');
   211→            }}
   212→          />
   213→
   214→          <CardActivity
   215→            mt="nano"
   216→            icon="icon-contact"
   217→            title="Demais localidades"
   218→            description="0800 015 0252"
   219→            iconRight="icon-next"
   220→            showIcon
   221→            showTagLabel={false}
   222→            onPress={() => {
   223→              eventSelectContent({
   224→                content_type: 'cta',
   225→                content_id: 'telefones_demais_localidades',
   226→                location_1: 'monetizacao',
   227→                location_2: 'hub_recompensas_spmais',
   228→                location_3: 'contato_telefones',
   229→              });
   230→
   231→              Linking.openURL('tel:08000150252');
   232→            }}
   233→          />
   234→          <Text type="paragraphXS" color="INTENSE_COLOR_04" mt="xxxxs">
   235→            *Atendimento de segunda à sexta das 9h às 21h. Sábado e domingo das
   236→            9h às 17h.
   237→          </Text>
   238→        </Box>
   239→
   240→        <Box align="center" width="100%" mt="xxxxs">
   241→          <Text
   242→            type="linkMDM"
   243→            color="INTENSE_COLOR_05"
   244→            onPress={handleCloseReportProblem}
   245→            align="center"
   246→          >
   247→            Fechar
   248→          </Text>
   249→        </Box>
   250→      </BackDropBottom>
   251→    </>
   252→  );
   253→};
   254→
   255→export default PointsDetailView;
   256→

content.content:
1→# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2→
     3→Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4→This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5→
     6→> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11→
    12→- [Introduction](#introduction)
    13→- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    14→- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    15→- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    16→- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    17→- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    18→- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    19→- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    20→- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    21→- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    22→- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    23→- [Checklist](#checklist)
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→## Introduction
    28→
    29→The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    30→
    31→Key decisions:
    32→
    33→- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    34→- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    35→- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    36→- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    37→
    38→---
    39→
    40→## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
    41→
    42→Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    43→
    44→```
    45→screens/Home/
    46→├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    47→├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    48→└── Models/
    49→    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    50→```
    51→
    52→**Models/index.d.ts**
    53→
    54→```ts
    55→export interface HomeParams {
    56→  welcome?: string;
    57→}
    58→export interface HomeState {
    59→  greeting: string;
    60→  isLoading: boolean;
    61→}
    62→export interface HomeHandlers {
    63→  onPressCTA: () => void;
    64→}
    65→export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    66→```
    67→
    68→**index.ts (logic hook)**
    69→
    70→```ts
    71→import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from 'react';
    72→import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from './Models';
    73→import { useUserStore } from '@/services/user/store'; // Zustand slice example
    74→
    75→export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    76→  const name = useUserStore(s => s.name);
    77→  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    78→
    79→  useEffect(() => {
    80→    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    81→    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    82→  }, []);
    83→
    84→  const greeting = useMemo(
    85→    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || 'guest'}!`,
    86→    [
    87→      params?.welcome,
    88→      name,
    89→    ],
    90→  );
    91→
    92→  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    93→    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    94→  }, []);
    95→
    96→  return {
    97→    greeting,
    98→    isLoading,
    99→    onPressCTA,
   100→  };
   101→}
   102→```
   103→
   104→**view.tsx (render)**
   105→
   106→```tsx
   107→import React from 'react';
   108→import { View } from 'react-native';
   109→import { Text, Button } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
   110→import { useHomeScreen } from './index';
   111→import type { HomeParams } from './Models';
   112→
   113→type Props = HomeParams;
   114→
   115→export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   116→  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   117→
   118→  if (isLoading) {
   119→    return (
   120→      <View>
   121→        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   122→      </View>
   123→    );
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  return (
   127→    <View>
   128→      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   129→      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   130→    </View>
   131→  );
   132→}
   133→```
   134→
   135→---
   136→
   137→## Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern

   144→    └── index.d.ts
   145→```

content.text:
Review this Storybook story file as if it were a pull request. The task is SP_0004.2 — rendering the PointsDetail screen in Storybook.

Context:
- This is a React Native MFE; Storybook runs via @storybook/react-native-web-vite
- BasicScreen and BackDropBottom can't be imported (SafeAreaView, Modalize native deps) — manual recreation is the pattern
- The screen shows transaction details with different states based on transaction type
- Real utils from src/screens/PointsDetail/utils.ts are reused (pure functions)
- DS components from @semparar/design-system-app are used directly

Review the file at: /Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/PointsDetailScreen.stories.tsx

Check for:
1. Code quality and consistency with existing stories (ExtractScreen.stories.tsx, PointsScreen.stories.tsx)
2. Missing states or edge cases
3. Potential runtime issues
4. Type safety
5. Story metadata completeness

Be concise. List issues as bullet points with severity (blocking/minor/nit).

1→import React, { useState } from 'react';
     2→import { ScrollView } from 'react-native';
     3→import type { Meta, StoryObj } from '@storybook/react';
     4→import {
     5→  Box,
     6→  Button,

    14→import { NavigationDecorator } from '../../.storybook/decorators/NavigationDecorator';
    15→import type { PointsTransaction } from '../services/models/extract.model';
    16→import { formatToCurrency } from '../utils/functions';
    17→import {
    18→  getDetailScreenTitle,
    19→  getFormattedHighlightText,
    20→  getFormattedPointsValue,
    21→  getOrigin,

    23→  getTagSubType,
    24→  getTransactionGainType,
    25→  getVehicleInfo,
    26→} from '../screens/PointsDetail/utils';
    27→import { moment } from '../services/moment';
    28→

    44→    ],
    45→  },

    47→};
    48→
    49→const MOCK_CARD_TRANSACTION: PointsTransaction = {
    50→  id: 'card-1',
    51→  date: '2026-02-18T10:15:00Z',
    52→  description: 'Compra via cartão',
    53→  type: 'CREDIT',
    54→  amount: 320,
    55→  currencyValue: 45.9,
    56→  icon: 'icon-credit-card',
    57→  details: {
    58→    provider: 'Loja XYZ',
    59→    extraInfo: '',
    60→    fields: [{ label: 'Origem', value: 'Cartão Sem Parar Mais' }],
    61→  },
    62→  status: 'CONFIRMED',
    63→};
    64→
    65→const MOCK_CASHBACK_CREDIT: PointsTransaction = {
    66→  id: 'cashback-1',
    67→  date: '2026-02-15T16:45:00Z',
    68→  description: 'Cashback na fatura',
    69→  type: 'CREDIT',
    70→  amount: 500,
    71→  currencyValue: 5.0,
    72→  icon: 'icon-invoice',
    73→  details: {
    74→    provider: 'Cashback Sem Parar',
    75→    extraInfo: '',
    76→    fields: [],
    77→  },
    78→  status: 'CONFIRMED',
    79→};
    80→
    81→const MOCK_CASHBACK_RESCUE: PointsTransaction = {
    82→  id: 'cashback-2',
    83→  date: '2026-02-12T09:00:00Z',
    84→  description: 'Desconto na fatura',
    85→  type: 'RESCUE',
    86→  amount: 1000,
    87→  currencyValue: 10.0,
    88→  icon: 'icon-invoice',
    89→  details: {
    90→    provider: 'Cashback Sem Parar',
    91→    extraInfo: '',
    92→    fields: [],
    93→  },
    94→  status: 'CONFIRMED',
    95→};
    96→
    97→const MOCK_EXPIRED: PointsTransaction = {
    98→  id: 'expired-1',
    99→  date: '2026-01-10T00:00:00Z',
   100→  description: 'Pontos expirados',
   101→  type: 'EXPIRED',
   102→  amount: 800,
   103→  currencyValue: 8.0,
   104→  icon: 'icon-less',
   105→  details: {
   106→    provider: '',
   107→    extraInfo: '',
   108→    fields: [],
   109→  },
   110→  status: 'EXPIRED',
   111→};
   112→
   113→const MOCK_REFUNDED: PointsTransaction = {
   114→  id: 'refunded-1',
   115→  date: '2026-02-08T11:30:00Z',
   116→  description: 'Estorno voucher abastecimento',
   117→  type: 'REFUNDED',
   118→  amount: 250,
   119→  currencyValue: 2.5,
   120→  icon: 'icon-transfer',
   121→  details: {
   122→    provider: 'Sem Parar',
   123→    extraInfo: '',
   124→    fields: [],
   125→  },
   126→  status: 'REFUNDED',
   127→};
   128→
   129→const TRANSACTIONS_MAP: Record<string, PointsTransaction> = {
   130→  'Tag (Pedágio)': MOCK_TAG_TRANSACTION,
   131→  'Card Purchase': MOCK_CARD_TRANSACTION,
   132→  'Cashback Credit': MOCK_CASHBACK_CREDIT,
   133→  'Cashback Rescue': MOCK_CASHBACK_RESCUE,
   134→  Expired: MOCK_EXPIRED,
   135→  Refunded: MOCK_REFUNDED,
   136→};
   137→
   138→function Divider() {
   139→  return (
   140→    <SeparatorHorizontal
   141→      borderWidth="smaller"
   142→      borderColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02"
   143→      height={1}
   144→      width="100%"
   145→      mt="xxxxs"
   146→    />
   147→  );
   148→}
   149→
   150→function ReportProblemModal({ onClose }: { onClose: () => void }) {
   151→  return (
   152→    <Box
   153→      bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03"
   154→      pd="innerSmall"
   155→      style={{
   156→        borderTopLeftRadius: 16,
   157→        borderTopRightRadius: 16,
   158→        borderWidth: 1,
   159→        borderColor: '#e0e0e0',
   160→      }}
   161→    >
   162→      <Box align="center" width="100%" mt="xxs">
   163→        <Text
   164→          type="paragraphXXXL"
   165→          color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
   166→          mb="xs"
   167→          align="center"
   168→        >
   169→          Central de Atendimento
   170→        </Text>
   171→      </Box>
   172→
   173→      <CardActivity
   174→        mt="nano"
   175→        icon="icon-contact"
   176→        title="Capitais"
   177→        description="4002 1552"
   178→        iconRight="icon-next"
   179→        showIcon
   180→        showTagLabel={false}
   181→        onPress={() => {}}
   182→      />
   183→
   184→      <CardActivity
   185→        mt="nano"
   186→        icon="icon-contact"
   187→        title="Demais localidades"
   188→        description="0800 015 0252"
   189→        iconRight="icon-next"
   190→        showIcon
   191→        showTagLabel={false}
   192→        onPress={() => {}}
   193→      />
   194→
   195→      <Text type="paragraphXS" color="INTENSE_COLOR_04" mt="xxxxs">
   196→        *Atendimento de segunda à sexta das 9h às 21h. Sábado e domingo das 9h
   197→        às 17h.
   198→      </Text>
   199→
   200→      <Box align="center" width="100%" mt="xxxxs">
   201→        <Text
   202→          type="linkMDM"
   203→          color="INTENSE_COLOR_05"
   204→          onPress={onClose}
   205→          align="center"
   206→        >
   207→          Fechar
   208→        </Text>
   209→      </Box>
   210→    </Box>
   211→  );
   212→}
   213→
   214→function PointsDetailStoryWrapper({
   215→  transactionType = 'Tag (Pedágio)',
   216→}: {
   217→  transactionType?: string;
   218→}) {
   219→  const [showModal, setShowModal] = useState(false);
   220→  const transaction = TRANSACTIONS_MAP[transactionType] || MOCK_TAG_TRANSACTION;
   221→
   222→  const gainType = getTransactionGainType(transaction);
   223→  const tagSubType = getTagSubType(transaction);
   224→  const screenTitle = getDetailScreenTitle(gainType, transaction.type);
   225→  const vehicleInfo = getVehicleInfo(transaction);
   226→  const purchaseTypeLabel = getPurchaseTypeLabel(tagSubType);
   227→  const origin = getOrigin(transaction);
   228→  const formattedCurrencyValue = formatToCurrency(transaction.currencyValue);
   229→  const formattedPointsValue = getFormattedPointsValue(
   230→    transaction.amount,
   231→    transaction.type,
   232→  );
   233→  const cardTitle = transaction.description || '';
   234→  const highlightText = getFormattedHighlightText(
   235→    transaction.date,
   236→    gainType,
   237→    moment,
   238→  );
   239→
   240→  const hasVehicleInfo = !!vehicleInfo && vehicleInfo !== '';
   241→  const shouldShowOrigin = origin && !hasVehicleInfo;
   242→  const hasPurchaseTypeLabel =
   243→    !!purchaseTypeLabel && purchaseTypeLabel !== '';
   244→
   245→  return (
   246→    <Box bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03" flex={1}>
   247→      <Header
   248→        title={screenTitle}
   249→        iconLeft="icon-back"
   250→        hideLeftIcon={false}
   251→        onBack={() => {}}
   252→      />
   253→      <ScrollView contentContainerStyle={{ flexGrow: 1 }}>
   254→        <DetailTransactionValue
   255→          icon={transaction.icon as never}
   256→          title={cardTitle}
   257→          titleColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
   258→          iconBgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03"
   259→          points={formattedPointsValue}
   260→          bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02"
   261→          pointsColor={
   262→            transaction.type === 'CREDIT' || transaction.type === 'REFUNDED'
   263→              ? 'PLUS_COLOR_02'
   264→              : ('INTENSE_COLOR_05' as const)
   265→          }
   266→        />
   267→
   268→        <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller" mt="xxxxs" flex={1}>
   269→          <TagLabel
   270→            icon={undefined}
   271→            type="neutral"
   272→            text={highlightText}
   273→            showSpacing
   274→          />
   275→
   276→          <Box mt="xxxs" flex={1} width="100%">
   277→            <Text type="paragraphXL" color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01" mb="xxxxs">
   278→              Valor
   279→            </Text>
   280→            <Text type="paragraphMD" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03">
   281→              {formattedCurrencyValue}
   282→            </Text>
   283→
   284→            {gainType === 'TAG' && (
   285→              <>
   286→                <Divider />
   287→                {shouldShowOrigin && (
   288→                  <>
   289→                    <Text
   290→                      type="paragraphXL"
   291→                      color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
   292→                      mb="xxxxs"
   293→                      mt="xxxxs"
   294→                    >
   295→                      Origem
   296→                    </Text>
   297→                    <Text type="paragraphMD" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03">
   298→                      {origin}
   299→                    </Text>
   300→                    <Divider />
   301→                  </>
   302→                )}
   303→                {hasVehicleInfo && (
   304→                  <>
   305→                    <Text
   306→                      type="paragraphXL"
   307→                      color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
   308→                      mb="xxxxs"
   309→                      mt="xxxxs"
   310→                    >
   311→                      Tag
   312→                    </Text>
   313→                    <Text type="paragraphMD" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03">
   314→                      {vehicleInfo}
   315→                    </Text>
   316→                    <Divider />
   317→                  </>
   318→                )}
   319→                {hasPurchaseTypeLabel && (
   320→                  <>
   321→                    <Text
   322→                      type="paragraphXL"
   323→                      color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
   324→                      mb="xxxxs"
   325→                      mt="xxxxs"
   326→                    >
   327→                      Tipo de compra
   328→                    </Text>
   329→                    <Text type="paragraphMD" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03">
   330→                      {purchaseTypeLabel}
   331→                    </Text>
   332→                  </>
   333→                )}
   334→              </>
   335→            )}
   336→          </Box>
   337→
   338→          <Box width="100%" align="flex-end" mb="small">
   339→            <Button
   340→              type="secundaryLarge"
   341→              text="Relatar Problema"
   342→              onPress={() => setShowModal(true)}
   343→            />
   344→          </Box>
   345→        </Box>
   346→
   347→        {showModal && (
   348→          <ReportProblemModal onClose={() => setShowModal(false)} />
   349→        )}
   350→      </ScrollView>
   351→    </Box>
   352→  );
   353→}
   354→
   355→const meta: Meta<typeof PointsDetailStoryWrapper> = {
   356→  title: 'Screens/PointsDetail',
   357→  component: PointsDetailStoryWrapper,
   358→  decorators: [NavigationDecorator],
   359→  argTypes: {
   360→    transactionType: {
   361→      control: { type: 'select' },
   362→      options: Object.keys(TRANSACTIONS_MAP),
   363→      description: 'Transaction type variant to display',
   364→    },
   365→  },
   366→};
   367→
   368→export default meta;
   369→type Story = StoryObj<typeof PointsDetailStoryWrapper>;
   370→

   372→  name: 'Tag Transaction (Pedágio)',
   373→  args: { transactionType: 'Tag (Pedágio)' },
